entities.name: PEARL MILLET
entities.description: The host crop Pennisetum glaucum [P. americanum] susceptible to downy mildew

entities.name: SCLEROSPORA GRAMINICOLA
entities.description: The fungal pathogen that causes downy mildew in pearl millet

entities.name: TEMPERATURE
entities.description: Environmental factor that influences sporangial germination, zoospore viability, and infectivity

entities.name: LIGHT
entities.description: Environmental factor studied for its effect on zoospore infectivity and downy mildew development

entities.name: SPORANGIAL GERMINATION
entities.description: The process of zoospore release from the sporangia of Sclerospora graminicola

entities.name: ZOOSPORE VIABILITY
entities.description: The ability of zoospores to remain alive and functional over time

entities.name: GERM TUBE GROWTH
entities.description: The growth of germ tubes following the germination of sporangia

entities.name: ZOOSPORE INFECTIVITY
entities.description: The capacity of zoospores to cause systemic infection in the host plant

entities.name: DOWNY MILDEW DEVELOPMENT
entities.description: The progression and incidence of the disease caused by Sclerospora graminicola

entities.name: 30 DEGREES CELSIUS
entities.description: The specific temperature at which sporangial germination and zoospore infectivity were highest

entities.name: ROOTS
entities.description: The underground part of young pearl millet seedlings susceptible to infection

entities.name: SHOOTS
entities.description: The above-ground part of young pearl millet seedlings susceptible to infection
